data.title: Caris Life Sciences sees Q4 total revenue of about $281M
data.description:
• Caris Life Sciences (CAI) on Monday said it sees Q4 total revenue of approximately $281 million, an increase of approximately 116%.
  • The revenue consist of approximately $270 million of Molecular Profiling services revenue and approximately $11 million of Pharma research and developmental services revenue.
  • Full year 2025 preliminary unaudited financial results: Total revenue of approximately $800 million, an increase of approximately 94%.
  • Consisting of approximately $755 million of Molecular Profiling services revenue (including approximately $33 million of prior year true-ups) and approximately $45 million of Pharma research and developmental services revenue.
  • Preliminary unaudited cash, cash equivalents, restricted cash and marketable securities were about $802 million as of December 31, 2025, a net increase of about $43 million from September 30, 2025.
  • Consensus for FY25 and Q4 revenues are $723.42M and $204.31M.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: AbbVie in up to $5.6B deal with RemeGen for bispecific antibody for solid tumors
data.description:
• AbbVie (ABBV) has inked a licensing deal with China-based RemeGen for the latter's RC148, a PD-1/VEGF-targeted bispecific antibody under development for advanced solid tumors.
  • Given that early studies have indicated initial favorable antitumor activity of RC148 in combination with an antibody-drug conjugate, AbbVie said that it could potentially be used with its own investigational ADC, telisotuzumab adizutecan (Temab-A).
  • Terms call for AbbVie have exclusive rights to develop, manufacture, and commercialize RC148 outside of the Greater China area. RemeGen will receive an upfront payment of $650M plus potential milestone payments of $4.95B, as well as tiered, double-digit royalties on sales outside of Greater China.

data.title: Nuvation Bio says preliminary Q4 and FY25 IBTROZI net product revenue was $15.7M and $24.7M
data.description:
• Nuvation Bio (NUVB) on Monday said preliminary fourth quarter and full year 2025 IBTROZI net product revenue was $15.7 million and $24.7 million, respectively.
  • Received $25 million milestone payment from Nippon Kayaku as a result of establishment of the reimbursement price in Japan in Q4.
  • Preliminary unaudited cash, cash equivalents and marketable securities are expected to be approximately $529.2 million as of December 31, 2025
  • NUVB -2.3% premarket to $7.855.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: ClearPoint Neuro announces preliminary unaudited fourth quarter and full year 2025 revenue
data.description:
• ClearPoint Neuro (CLPT) on Monday reported preliminary unaudited revenue of approximately $10.4 million for the fourth quarter of 2025, a 20% increase from the same period in 2024.
  • Full-year 2025 revenue is estimated at $37 million.
  • The company expects 2026 revenue between $54 million and $60 million, driven by organic growth and integration of its IRRAflow product line. 
  • Audited results will be released in the first quarter of 2026.
  • CLPT +5.29% premarket to $15.71.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Establishment Labs reports preliminary Q4 revenue between $64M to $65M
data.description:
• Establishment Labs (ESTA) said on Monday that its preliminary unaudited revenue for the fourth quarter of 2025 is expected to be in the range of $64.0 million to $65.0 million, including $17.0 million to $17.5 million from Motiva sales in the United States.
  • Based on the expected range for fourth quarter revenue, Establishment Labs expects full-year 2025 revenue of $210.5 million to $211.5 million, an increase of approximately 27% over 2024 including between $45.4 million and $45.9 million from Motiva sales in the United States. .
  • The company’s year-end 2025 cash position is expected to be approximately $75.5 million
  • ESTA +8.29% premarket to $74.01.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Applied Materials, Advanced Energy in focus as Susquehanna upgrades on semi strength
data.description:
Applied Materials (AMAT) and Advanced Energy Industries (AEIS) were in focus on Monday as Susquehanna upgraded the pair on continued semiconductor equipment spending strength.

Applied Materials and Advanced Energy Industries were slightly higher in premarket trading.

“Recent checks suggest the [semiconductor capital equipment] industry is preparing for $120 billion [wafer fab equipment] spending,” analyst Mehdi Hosseini wrote in a note to clients. “Additionally, while supply chain bottlenecks make the path to $150 billion volatile, the bigger picture is often overlooked: with global semi revenues trending to [more than] $1 trillion, [wafer fab equipment] supply chain must prepare for spending well above $150 billion! In short, although we believe the journey toward $150 billion-plus will be choppy, the underlying fundamental trends remain strong. In our view, company-specific earnings are poised to grow at [more than] 25% CAGR, with the cycle duration extending for several years. Combined with the fact that SCE companies deliver double-digit FCF margins, we believe this also supports an uplift in valuation multiples.”

Hosseini upgraded Applied Materials to Positive from Neutral and upped his price target to $400 from $180. Advanced Energy Industries was raised to Positive from Neutral and the price target was raised to $300 from $190.

In addition to the upgrades, Hosseini raised the price targets on ASML (ASML), KLA Corp. (KLAC), Lam Research (LRCX) and Teradyne (TER).

data.title: Lightwave Logic appoints Aref Chowdhury as chief technology officer
data.description:
• Lightwave Logic (LWLG) has appointed Dr. Aref Chowdhury as its new chief technology officer, effective January 1, 2026.
  • Most recently, Chowdhury served as vice president strategy and chief technology officer of Network Infrastructure for Nokia.
  • Chowdhury will be focused on executing the cCompany's technology strategy to accelerate commercialization timelines while ensuring product innovation, scalability, reliability, and competitiveness.
  • LWLG +1.20% premarket to $4.2.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: American Eagle Outfitters raises Q4 operating income on solid holiday sales
data.description:
• American Eagle (AEO) raised its fourth quarter operating income outlook as the company saw a high-single-digit increase in comparable sales for the final quarter of the year.
  • The retailer now expects operating income to be within a range of $167M and $170M from a prior range of $155M and $160M, reflecting “solid margin performance” and the anticipation for Q4 comparable sales to be up 8% to 9%.
  • These assumptions also include ~$50M of tariff headwinds.
  • “Momentum continued in the fourth quarter with record December sales fueled by the power of our brands, with particularly strong growth at Aerie and Offline and sequential growth at American Eagle,” said the company’s CEO, Jay Schottenstein.
  • The updated guidance comes ahead of the company’s 2:00pm presentation at the ICR Conference in Orlando, Florida.

data.title: Apyx Medical reports preliminary Q4 revenue up about 34%
data.description:
• Apyx Medical Corporation (APYX) on Monday reported reported preliminary, unaudited fourth-quarter revenue in the range of approximately $19.0 to $19.2 million, up about 34% from a year earlier.
  • For the full year, preliminary revenue is expected to be in the range of approximately $52.7 to $52.9 million, up around 10% year-over-year.
  • Surgical Aesthetics revenue grew about 17% and is expected to be in the range of $45.2 to $45.4 million, while OEM revenue declined roughly 21% to $7.5 million.
  • The company noted that momentum from the commercial launch of its AYON Body Contouring System contributed to the strong fourth-quarter performance.
  • Final audited results for 2025 are expected in March.
  • APYX +1.71% premarket to $3.55.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Elutia sees Q4 net sales of $3.3M
data.description:
• Elutia (ELUT) on Monday said for the fourth quarter of 2025, preliminary net sales were approximately $3.3 million, representing a 16% increase compared to the fourth quarter of 2024.
  • Net sales for both the fourth quarters of 2025 and 2024 exclude contributions from the divested BioEnvelope business.
  • Consensus for Q4 revenue is $3.10M.
  • During the fourth quarter, the company repaid the full $26.9 million outstanding principal, accrued interest, and accrued exit fees associated with its loan from SWK Holdings, significantly reducing interest expense.
  • As of December 31, 2025, cash on hand was about $36.3 million. An additional $8.0 million related to the BioEnvelope divestiture is being held in escrow and is expected to be released during 2026.
  • As of December 31, 2025, the company had 42.8 million shares of Class A common stock outstanding, 4.5 million pre-funded warrants to purchase shares of Class A common stock, and no Class B common stock outstanding.
  • ELUT +3.73% premarket to $0.75.
  • Source: Press Release
